persona.name: River Achebe-Chen

occupation.title: Freelance Illustrator and Muralist
occupation.organization: Self-employed; commissions from Portland nonprofits, local restaurants, and community art organizations
occupation.description: Creates large-scale murals and editorial illustration for community spaces across Portland's east side. Income is variable and project-based. Has painted three murals in 2025 so far and illustrated a zine for a local mutual aid network. Considers shoe aesthetics a form of wearable public art — takes it seriously as visual culture, not just commerce.

persona.education: B.F.A. Illustration, Pacific Northwest College of Art (PNCA)
long_term_goals: Get a major commission for a building-scale mural in Portland's central city
Build enough financial stability to stop taking projects they're not excited about
Contribute to a shoe brand's creative direction in a way that feels authentic
Travel to Lagos and Guangzhou to connect with both sides of their family
persona.style: Expressive and spontaneous — describes things in terms of energy and feeling before specs or function. Interrupts themselves with tangents and always circles back. Uses they/them pronouns. Speaks in short, vivid sentences when excited; gets longer and more thoughtful when challenged or uncertain. The way they dress is highly intentional — the shoe is the anchor piece in most outfits and is selected for how it speaks, not how it performs.

traits: Attracted to novelty and cultural crossover
Protective of cultural authenticity — notices when brands do and don't understand what they're referencing

interests: Mural art and large-scale public illustration
Sneaker aesthetics and their role as visual culture objects
likes: Unexpected, culturally layered colorways that tell a story
Sustainable or recycled material constructions that don't hide their ethics behind boring design
Running shoe silhouettes that hold visual interest from multiple angles
Brands that sponsor queer and BIPOC athletes
Flat-lay sneaker photography — has a dedicated Instagram account for it
Finding unusual shoes at Powell's Books gear swap or local consignment
dislikes: Hyper-masculine running shoe marketing that treats performance as the only valid frame
Brands that appropriate African or Asian visual motifs without credit or collaboration
Heavy, clunky shoes with no visual lightness
Shoes that photograph poorly — material, silhouette, and color all matter in 2D
Any brand whose social values are purely performative — they can usually tell

beliefs: Art is embedded in everything we make and wear — calling a shoe 'just a shoe' misses something real
Cultural reference in design should earn its way through understanding, not extraction
Identity is fluid; what you wear should have room to be too
Community support for artists is not charity — it's infrastructure
skills: Sneaker flat-lay photography and short-form creative content
Community arts facilitation and collaborative creative projects

general: Owns 10+ pairs of shoes; considers each purchase a considered visual statement
Researches sneaker releases through Instagram discovery and community channels more than formal retail sources
Evaluates new shoes by photographing them in various light conditions before deciding
Rarely runs for fitness but walks and skates everywhere; comfort matters more than they vocalize

morning: Coffee and 30–40 minutes of Instagram scrolling, heavy on art and sneaker accounts
Studio work or site prep by 11am when on a mural project
workday: Mural or illustration work in long, intensive blocks when in project mode
Admin and invoicing on slow days, usually avoided until necessary
Skates or walks to project sites within 3 miles
evening: Dinner at a community spot in Alberta Arts District — usually something cheap and good
Community events, gallery openings, or music 2–3 nights per week
Late nights common; irregular sleep schedule is an ongoing issue
weekend: Swap meets and vintage markets Sunday morning
Group skate or casual walk with queer creative friend group
Works on personal illustration projects or zine content Saturday evenings

health.physical: Healthy and active through daily walking and skating; no structured exercise routine. Good energy. Irregular eating patterns from freelance unpredictability. Sleep is the most variable health factor — late creative hours followed by early site calls.
health.mental: Creative work is emotionally regulatory for River — making something visible makes the inner world manageable. Income uncertainty causes periodic anxiety that they manage with community connection and creative output. Long stretches between paid commissions are harder than they admit.

relationships.name: Sasha
relationships.description: Queer visual artist in Portland. They share a studio several days a week and collaborate on zine projects. Sasha is often the first person River shows a new shoe purchase to.

relationships.name: Nia Achebe
relationships.description: Lives in Seattle; Nigerian-American community organizer. Calls weekly and supports River's creative career with active pride even when she doesn't fully understand the projects.

relationships.name: Jin
relationships.description: Grew up in Portland, introduced River to serious sneaker culture three years ago. Jin's opinion on a new shoe is the first external check River applies.
